 SKL_MP4_ENC ()

SKL_MP4_ENC * Skl_MP4_New_Encoder ()
void Skl_MP4_Delete_Encoder (SKL_MP4_ENC *)

Detailed Description

SKL_MP4_ENC is the main encoding class. It is responsible for the bitstream coding only (that is: enforcing the syntax). The motion estimation is performed by an external module (SKL_MP4_ANALYZER), and parameter exchange is done using Get_Param().

All methods are virtual, just like the COM-object paradigm. It eases the dynamic class loading (just map the proxy's symbol from dll, the vtbl will come along).

See also:
SKL_MP4_ANALYZER.

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

SKL_MP4_ENC::SKL_MP4_ENC (   )  [protected]
 

protected constructor. Use Skl_MP4_New_Encoder()

virtual SKL_MP4_ENC::~SKL_MP4_ENC (   )  [protected, virtual]
 

protected destructor. use Skl_MP4_Delete_Encoder()

Member Function Documentation

virtual int SKL_MP4_ENC::Encode (   )  [pure virtual]
 

Encode next input frame, as set up by call to Prepare_Next_Frame.

Returns:
the number of coded bytes available for writting in the buffer is returned.
See also:
Get_Bits, Get_Bits_Length, Prepare_Next_Frame.

virtual int SKL_MP4_ENC::Finish_Encoding (   )  [pure virtual]
 

Mark the end of sequence in the bitstream. Returns number of coded bytes.

See also:
Get_Bits, Get_Bits_Length.

virtual SKL_MP4_ANALYZER* SKL_MP4_ENC::Get_Analyzer (   )  const [pure virtual]
 

Returns the analyzer currently associated with the encoder.

See also:
Set_analyzer

virtual const SKL_BYTE* SKL_MP4_ENC::Get_Bits (   )  const [pure virtual]
 

Returns the pointer to the coded bytes after last call to Encode(). This pointer and its content is only valid until next call to Encode(). The number of bytes is available using the Get_Bits_Length() member function.

See also:
Encode, Get_Bits_Length

virtual int SKL_MP4_ENC::Get_Bits_Length (   )  const [pure virtual]
 

Returns the number of coded bytes after last call to Encode(). This value is only valid until next call to Encode(). The pointer to these bytes is available using the Get_Bits() member function.

See also:
Encode, Get_Bits

virtual const SKL_MP4_PIC* SKL_MP4_ENC::Get_Last_Coded_Frame (   )  const [pure virtual]
 

Return the last coded frame, if any. This data is only valid until next call to Encode() Warning : beware of the SKL_MP4_PIC data layout!

Returns:
Last coded frame.
See also:
SKL_MP4_PIC

virtual const SKL_MP4_PIC* SKL_MP4_ENC::Get_Next_Frame (   )  const [pure virtual]
 

Return the last input frame set up by previous call to Prepare_Next_Frame(). This data is only valid until next call to Encode() Warning : beware of the SKL_MP4_PIC data layout!

Returns:
Last available input frame.
See also:
SKL_MP4_PIC

virtual int SKL_MP4_ENC::Ioctl (  SKL_CST_STRING  Param  )  [pure virtual]
 

This method can be used for very special syntax controls over the bitstream.

Parameters:
Param can be one of the following constant strings:
  • "emit-key-headers": for streaming purpose, the bitstream will contain a VOL header every time a keyframe is transmitted.
  • "no-emit-key-headers": disables the above (default).
  • "emit-sequence-codes": the bitstream will contain a SEQUENCE_START and SEQUENCE_END codes at the beginning and the end.
  • "no-sequence-codes": disables the above (default).
Note: Call to this method should occur preferably at the beginning of the encoding process.

virtual const SKL_MP4_PIC* SKL_MP4_ENC::Prepare_Next_Frame (  int  Width,
int  Height
)  [pure virtual]
 

Set the dimensions of the next input frame. A SKL_MP4_PIC structure is returned, ready for holding fresh data.

Warning : beware of the SKL_MP4_PIC data layout!

Parameters:
Width the width, in pixel, of the input picture.
Height the height, in pixel, of the input picture.
Returns:
This functions returns a uninitialized picture structure SKL_MP4_PIC, ready to be filled with incoming YUV data.
See also:
SKL_MP4_PIC

virtual SKL_MP4_ANALYZER* SKL_MP4_ENC::Set_Analyzer (  SKL_MP4_ANALYZER *  Analyzer = 0  )  [pure virtual]
 

This function plugs a new analyzer code in replacement of the previous one.

Returns:
Set_Analyzer() returns previous analyzer.
Parameters:
Analyzer Should be 0 to restore the default, built-in, analyzer.
See also:
Get_analyzer

SKL_MP4_ANALYZER

virtual void SKL_MP4_ENC::Set_CPU (  SKL_CPU_FEATURE  Cpu = SKL_CPU_DETECT  )  [pure virtual]
 

Sets the CPU feature to use.

Parameters:
Cpu : see the enum description for a list of available cpu features.

virtual void SKL_MP4_ENC::Set_Custom_Matrix (  int  Intra,
const SKL_BYTE *  M = 0
)  [pure virtual]
 

Sets the custom quantization matrix for MPEG4 quantization type (0).

Parameters:
Intra Should be 1 to use the custom matrix for intra macroblocks, or 0 for inter macroblocks.
M a pointer to 64 bytes containing the 8x8 matrix coefficients. If the pointer is null, the coefficients are restored to their default, initial, values.

virtual SKL_MEM_I* SKL_MP4_ENC::Set_Memory_Manager (  SKL_MEM_I *  Mem = 0  )  [pure virtual]
 

Set a new memory pool to use internally for subsequent memory allocation. Warning: it should preferably be called at start-up only, to ensure that cycles of New()/Delete() are performed on the same memory pool.

Parameters:
Mem The memory pool to use. If equal to 0 (default), the C++ heap will be used.
Returns:
This function will return the previous memory manager.
See also:
SKL_MEM_I

virtual void SKL_MP4_ENC::Set_Slicer (  SKL_MP4_SLICER  Slicer,
SKL_ANY  Slicer_Data = 0
)  [pure virtual]
 

Set post-coding hook function. The signature of this function is the following typedef:

typedef void (*SKL_MP4_SLICER)(const SKL_MP4_PIC *Pic, int y, int Height, SKL_ANY Data);

Parameters:
Slicer callback function to use
Slicer_Data can be anything, and will be passed as is to the Slicer hook function.
